evals.prompt: I just added a Dockerfile for a Python CLI project. I want the first narrow container check to confirm the image builds and the CLI entrypoint responds. Give me the exact commands and what to inspect afterward.
evals.expected_output: A concise response with Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect sections that uses one `docker build` command, one `docker run --rm` command, and points to stdout or one concrete artifact to inspect.

checks.text: The answer proposes one narrow smoke test that checks both the image build and CLI entrypoint response
The Build section includes a concrete `docker build` command
The Run section includes a concrete `docker run --rm` command
The answer includes the sections Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect

evals.prompt: I already have image `moltfarm-skillbuilder:verify`. I want to run one sandboxed check that writes run artifacts back to my working tree so I can inspect them on the host afterward. Give me the shortest exact command and tell me what host path to inspect.
evals.expected_output: A concise response with Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect sections that reuses the given image, uses a bind mount for host-visible artifacts, and names the host path to inspect after the run.

checks.text: The answer reuses the provided image instead of rebuilding it
The run command includes a bind mount and host-user mapping for host-visible writes
The Inspect section names a host-visible path rather than only saying stdout
The answer includes the sections Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect

evals.prompt: I already have image `moltfarm-skillbuilder:verify`. I just want the narrowest container check that `./molt skill-builder --help` prints usage. Do not add mounts unless they are required.
evals.expected_output: A concise response with Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect sections that reuses the provided image, uses one stdout-only `docker run --rm` command, and avoids unnecessary mounts.

checks.text: The answer reuses the provided image and does not rebuild it
The run command stays stdout-only and avoids bind mounts or extra options that are not required
The Run section includes one exact `docker run --rm` command for `./molt skill-builder --help`
The answer includes the sections Goal, Build, Run, Verify, and Inspect
